description: Online retailers, direct-to-consumer brands, marketplace sellers, and e-commerce technology companies. This fast-paced industry is characterized by data-driven decision making, rapid experimentation, and constant pressure to optimize customer acquisition costs and lifetime value.

pain_points.pain: Rising customer acquisition costs (CAC) eroding margins
pain_points.context: iOS 14.5 privacy changes and competition have doubled Facebook/Instagram CPMs for many brands

pain_points.pain: Attribution and measurement broken by privacy changes
pain_points.context: Can't accurately track which channels drive conversions; MMM and incrementality testing gaining interest
Stockouts lose sales; overstock ties up cash. Supply chain volatility makes planning difficult

pain_points.pain: Shipping and fulfillment costs cutting into margins
pain_points.context: Free shipping expectations while carrier rates increase; 3PL costs hard to control
Amazon takes 30-45% of revenue; Shopify payments and apps add up. Platform changes can kill businesses

pain_points.pain: Customer retention and repeat purchase rates
pain_points.context: First order often unprofitable; LTV depends on repeat purchases that many brands can't drive

pain_points.pain: Conversion rate optimization with limited traffic
pain_points.context: Small brands lack traffic for statistically significant A/B tests

buying_triggers: Preparing for peak season (Q4, Prime Day)
New funding round with growth mandate
iOS privacy changes breaking existing attribution
Customer acquisition costs hit unsustainable levels
Launching new product line or market expansion
budget_cycle: Often flexible with monthly or quarterly commitments preferred. May have annual budgets for larger operations. Peak spending aligned with Q4 holiday season preparation.
regulatory_concerns: Sales tax nexus and collection requirements

terminology.usage_context: Key revenue metric; upsells and bundles aim to increase AOV

terminology.definition: Total revenue expected from a customer over their entire relationship with the brand
terminology.usage_context: Critical for understanding allowable CAC; LTV:CAC ratio is a key health metric

terminology.definition: Total cost of acquiring a new customer, including advertising and marketing expenses
terminology.usage_context: The metric that keeps founders up at night; has risen dramatically post-iOS 14.5

terminology.definition: Percentage of website visitors who complete a purchase
terminology.usage_context: Industry average is 2-3%; small improvements have huge revenue impact

terminology.definition: When shoppers add items to cart but leave without purchasing
terminology.usage_context: Average rate is 70%+; recovery emails and retargeting aim to recapture lost sales

terminology.definition: Revenue generated per dollar spent on advertising
terminology.usage_context: Primary metric for evaluating ad performance; varies widely by industry and channel

terminology.definition: Total value of merchandise sold through a platform or marketplace
terminology.usage_context: Key metric for marketplaces; doesn't account for returns or cancellations

terminology.definition: Unique identifier for each distinct product and variant
terminology.usage_context: Inventory management and complexity; more SKUs = more operational burden

terminology.definition: Outsourced warehousing, fulfillment, and shipping services
terminology.usage_context: ShipBob, Deliverr, and others; enables scaling without owning infrastructure

terminology.definition: Process of receiving, processing, and delivering customer orders
terminology.usage_context: Major cost center; speed and accuracy impact customer experience and reviews

terminology.definition: Retail model where sellers don't hold inventory; products ship directly from supplier
terminology.usage_context: Low barrier to entry but thin margins and quality control challenges

terminology.definition: Recurring delivery business model with curated products
terminology.usage_context: Provides predictable revenue but churn management is critical

terminology.definition: Architecture separating frontend presentation from backend commerce functionality
terminology.usage_context: Enables custom experiences and omnichannel; typically for larger brands

terminology.definition: Modular approach assembling best-of-breed commerce components via APIs
terminology.usage_context: Alternative to monolithic platforms; provides flexibility but adds complexity

common_objections.objection: What's the ROI? Show me the incrementality, not just attributed revenue
common_objections.underlying_concern: Attribution is broken; brands have been burned by inflated ROAS claims that didn't translate to real revenue growth
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Offer incrementality testing or holdout methodology, share case studies with real P&L impact, discuss geo-lift testing capabilities, be honest about attribution limitations

common_objections.objection: How does this integrate with Shopify/our tech stack?
common_objections.underlying_concern: E-commerce operators use dozens of apps; anything that doesn't integrate seamlessly won't get adopted
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Demonstrate native Shopify app or integration, show Klaviyo/Attentive/other common integrations, discuss API capabilities, offer implementation support

common_objections.objection: I'm worried about platform lock-in - what if we leave Shopify?
common_objections.underlying_concern: Brands have seen Shopify fees increase and want to avoid dependency on any single vendor
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Discuss data portability and export capabilities, show multi-platform support (BigCommerce, WooCommerce), explain API-first architecture, avoid long-term contracts

common_objections.objection: We don't have time for a long implementation - peak season is coming
common_objections.underlying_concern: E-commerce moves fast; anything that takes more than a few weeks to implement misses the window
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Show rapid time-to-value with specific timelines, offer phased implementation, provide dedicated onboarding support, share fast-launch case studies

common_objections.objection: We're a small brand - is this built for companies our size?
common_objections.underlying_concern: Many e-commerce tools are built for enterprise and over-engineered for SMBs; pricing may not work at lower volumes
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Show SMB-specific pricing and packaging, share case studies from similar-sized brands, demonstrate simple UX, offer starter plans with room to grow

switching_barriers: Platform migrations are complex and risky
Active integrations across dozens of apps
SEO and traffic tied to existing URLs

scenario_templates.template: You're the {role} at a {revenue_range} DTC brand selling {product_category}. {trigger_event}. Your main concern is {pain_point}, and you need to make a decision before {deadline} to prepare for {season}.

You're a {role} running a {brand_stage} e-commerce brand. Your CAC has increased {percentage}% in the past year, and your LTV:CAC ratio is now {ratio}. {trigger_event}. You're evaluating {solution_category} tools.
You're the {role} at a brand that started on {platform} and is now doing ${revenue} annually. {trigger_event}. Your investors want {investor_expectation}, but your team is concerned about {concern}.
You're a {role} at an e-commerce company selling on both your own site and {marketplace}. {trigger_event}. You're trying to balance {priority_a} with {priority_b} while managing {constraint}.
You're the {role} at a {company_type} brand preparing for {event}. Last year you {last_year_result}. This year you need to {goal}, but your current {problem_area} is holding you back.
